human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for construction-specific procurement behavior, permit and inspection dependencies, site-safety and closeout boundaries, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to project procurement readiness, vendor constraint handling, shortage packaging, and site-aware availability assessment rather than generic purchasing or field execution.

Authority sources cover OSHA construction safety duties, FEMA procurement guidance, GSA contract administration, public permit and inspection workflow guidance, and federal construction submittal and closeout references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, permit gaps, shortages, vendor blockers, approval-control bypass requests, substitution pressure, incomplete inventory evidence, closeout blockers, safety-driven escalation, adjacent-lane rejection, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, and tenant-specific terminology normalization.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/construction-and-field-services/procurement-for-projects-specialist/ so it remains distinct from project controls, permitting, inspection, safety, superintendent, and licensed-trade lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated construction-and-field-services procurement-for-projects specialist for availability assessment, vendor constraint handling, shortage escalation, site-readiness validation, and closeout-aware boundary enforcement.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
